informal: Show that the lower limit topology $\mathbb{R}_l$ and $K$-topology $\mathbb{R}_K$ are not comparable.
lean:
theorem Munkres_exercise_13_6 :
  ¬ (∀ U, Rl.IsOpen U → K_topology.IsOpen U) ∧ ¬ (∀ U, K_topology.IsOpen U → Rl.IsOpen U)
reasoning: Two topologies being "not comparable" means neither is finer than the other, i.e., neither inclusion of open sets holds. The Lean statement asserts exactly this: ¬(every Rl-open is K-open) ∧ ¬(every K-open is Rl-open), which is the standard definition of incomparable topologies. The quantification over open sets U with the IsOpen predicates correctly captures the finer/coarser relation in both directions.
